name: Edo (Bini)
notes: Edo (Bini, Ẹdo), the major Edoid language of Edo State, Nigeria (Benin City); ~1.6 million speakers. STUB: created as the substrate anchor for the Gulf of Guinea Portuguese creoles — Hagemeijer (2011, 'The Gulf of Guinea creoles: genetic and typological relations', JPCL 26(1)) identifies Edo as the primary substrate of the proto-Gulf-of-Guinea creole (with Kikongo), source of the labial-velar stops /k͡p ɡ͡b/ and prenasalized onsets in cri/pre/aoa/fab. The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified: Edo uses a Latin orthography with ẹ, ọ, gb, kp, mw, rh, vb and tone marking conventions; no published letter-to-phoneme table was consulted, so no grapheme map is claimed. Edo is tonal (H, L, downstep).

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Edo (Benin City area).
